title: Hieracium cinderella
taxonomy: Hieracium cinderella is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family, which includes many flowering plant species. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Hieracium cinderella belonging to the subfamily Cichorioideae. The genus Hieracium is a group of plants commonly known as hawkweeds, which are native to Europe, Asia, and North America. The classification of Hieracium cinderella is also reflected in its botanical name, which indicates its relationship to other plants within the Asteraceae family. intro: Hieracium cinderella, also known as Cinderella hawkweed, is a plant species within the dandelion-like group of plants known as hawkweeds, characterized by its yellow flowers and toothed leaves. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium cinderella include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Hieracium cinderella is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in salves and ointments to treat skin issues and wounds.
